revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 小菅神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=葛飾区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[小菅]]3-1-2 |地図 = |祭神 =…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小菅神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=葛飾区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[小菅]]3-1-2 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''小菅神社'''（こすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[葛飾区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）に創建された。[[江戸]]東郊外の旧[[天領]]や旗本領によって構成された[[小菅県]]の守護神とすべく、[[伊勢神宮]]より分霊を勧請したものである。場所は小菅県庁内（現[[東京拘置所]]）に置かれた<ref name="神社">葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年、43-44p</ref>。
[[1871年]]（明治4年）、小菅県が廃止となったため、県庁内に置かれた神社は、地元の小菅村の鎮守だった田中稲荷神社に移設し、「小菅大神宮」に改称された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）、「小菅神社」に改称された<ref name="神社"></ref>。
元々あった田中稲荷神社は当社の[[摂社]]となった。田中稲荷神社は[[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に開村した小菅村の鎮守として創建され、[[正覚寺 (葛飾区小菅)|正覚寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった。なぜ「'''小菅'''稲荷神社」ではなく、「'''田中'''稲荷神社」なのかは謎である。一説によれば、勧請したのが田中氏だからといわれている<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[綾瀬駅]]より徒歩14分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こすけしんしや かつしかく}} [[Category:葛飾区の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 小菅神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=葛飾区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[小菅]]3-1-2 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''小菅神社'''（こすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[葛飾区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）に創建された。[[江戸]]東郊外の旧[[天領]]や旗本領によって構成された[[小菅県]]の守護神とすべく、[[伊勢神宮]]より分霊を勧請したものである。場所は小菅県庁内（現[[東京拘置所]]）に置かれた<ref name="神社">葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年、43-44p</ref>。
[[1871年]]（明治4年）、小菅県が廃止となったため、県庁内に置かれた神社は、地元の小菅村の鎮守だった田中稲荷神社に移設し、「小菅大神宮」に改称された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）、「小菅神社」に改称された<ref name="神社"></ref>。
元々あった田中稲荷神社は当社の[[摂社]]となった。田中稲荷神社は[[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に開村した小菅村の鎮守として創建され、[[正覚寺 (葛飾区小菅)|正覚寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった。なぜ「'''小菅'''稲荷神社」ではなく、「'''田中'''稲荷神社」なのかは謎である。一説によれば、勧請したのが田中氏だからといわれている<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[綾瀬駅]]より徒歩14分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年

元々あった田中稲荷神社は当社の[[摂社]]となった。田中稲荷神社は[[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に開村した小菅村の鎮守として創建され、[[正覚寺 (葛飾区小菅)|正覚寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった。なぜ「'''小菅'''稲荷神社」ではなく、「'''田中'''稲荷神社」なのかは謎である。一説によれば、勧請したのが田中氏だからといわれている<ref name="神社"></ref>。 <gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|田中稲荷神社 </gallery>

== 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）に創建された。[[江戸]]東郊外の旧[[天領]]や旗本領によって構成された[[小菅県]]の守護神とすべく、[[伊勢神宮]]より分霊を勧請したものである。場所は小菅県庁内（現[[東京拘置所]]）に置かれた<ref name="神社">葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年、43-44p</ref>。

{{神社 |名称 = 小菅神社 |画像=[[File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[小菅 (葛飾区)|小菅]]3-1-2 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''小菅神社'''（こすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[葛飾区]]の[[神社]]。 氏子地域は小菅1丁目〜3丁目。なお4丁目は[[香取神社 (葛飾区堀切)|上千葉香取神社]]の氏子にあたる。
== 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）に創建された。[[江戸]]東郊外の旧[[天領]]や旗本領によって構成された[[小菅県]]の守護神とすべく、[[伊勢神宮]]より分霊を勧請したものである。場所は小菅県庁内（現[[東京拘置所]]）に置かれた<ref name="神社">葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年、43-44p</ref>。
[[1871年]]（明治4年）、小菅県が廃止となったため、県庁内に置かれた神社は、地元の小菅村の鎮守だった田中稲荷神社に移設し、「小菅大神宮」に改称された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）、「小菅神社」に改称された<ref name="神社"></ref>。
元々あった田中稲荷神社は当社の[[摂社]]となった。田中稲荷神社は[[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に開村した小菅村の鎮守として創建され、[[正覚寺 (葛飾区小菅)|正覚寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった。なぜ「'''小菅'''稲荷神社」ではなく、「'''田中'''稲荷神社」なのかは謎である。一説によれば、勧請したのが田中氏だからといわれている<ref name="神社"></ref>。 <gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|田中稲荷神社 </gallery>
== 交通アクセス == *[[綾瀬駅]]より徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=経路案内}}）。

revision.text: [[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]]

[[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kosuge Shrine | native_name = 小菅神社 | image = [[File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG|250px]] | image_size = 250px | caption = Main shrine building | map_type = Japan Tokyo | coordinates = {{coord|35.75460|N|139.82123|E|region:JP_type:landmark}} | location = 3-1-2 Kosuge, [[Katsushika, Tokyo|Katsushika]], [[Tokyo]], Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-Ōmikami]] | established = [[1869]] (Meiji 2) | former_names = Kosuge Daijingū | affiliated_shrines = [[Tanaka Inari Shrine]] (subsidiary shrine) }}
'''Kosuge Shrine''' ({{nihongo|小菅神社|Kosuge Jinja}}) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kosuge, Katsushika Ward, Tokyo, Japan**. The shrine's **parishioners (ujiko) include residents of Kosuge 1-chome to 3-chome**, while **Kosuge 4-chome falls under the jurisdiction of Katori Shrine in Kamichiba**.
## **History** Kosuge Shrine was founded in **1869 (Meiji 2)** to serve as the **guardian shrine of Kosuge Prefecture** (小菅県), a short-lived administrative unit established in **the early Meiji era** to govern former **shogunate territories** and **hatamoto lands** in the eastern outskirts of Edo (modern Tokyo). The shrine was originally located **within the Kosuge Prefectural Office**, which was situated **at the site of today's Tokyo Detention House**.<ref name="katsushika1981">Katsushika City Board of Education, *Report on Shrines in Katsushika Ward*, 1981, pp. 43-44.</ref>
In **1871 (Meiji 4)**, after the **abolition of Kosuge Prefecture**, the shrine was **relocated** to the grounds of **Tanaka Inari Shrine**, which had been the **local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village** since the **Keichō era (1596–1615)**. The shrine was renamed **Kosuge Daijingū** (小菅大神宮). In **1909 (Meiji 42)**, it was officially renamed **Kosuge Shrine**.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
### **Tanaka Inari Shrine** The original **Tanaka Inari Shrine** (田中稲荷神社) became a **subsidiary shrine** (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as **Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity**, its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name **"Tanaka"** remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after **a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity**.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
## **Access** * **15-minute walk from Ayase Station** ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).
## **See Also** * [[Shinto]] * [[Tokyo Detention House]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Katori Shrine (Katsushika)]]
## **References** {{Reflist}}
## **External Links** * {{Commons category|Kosuge Shrine (Katsushika)}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine
| name = Kosuge Shrine
| native_name = 小菅神社
| image = [[File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG|250px]]
| image_size = 250px
| caption = Main shrine building
| map_type = Japan Tokyo
| coordinates = {{coord|35.75460|N|139.82123|E|region:JP_type:landmark}}
| location = 3-1-2 Kosuge, [[Katsushika, Tokyo|Katsushika]], [[Tokyo]], Japan
| deity = [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-Ōmikami]]
| established = [[1869]] (Meiji 2)
| former_names = Kosuge Daijingū
| affiliated_shrines = [[Tanaka Inari Shrine]] (subsidiary shrine)
}}
'''Kosuge Shrine''' ({{nihongo|小菅神社|Kosuge Jinja}}) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kosuge, Katsushika Ward, Tokyo, Japan'''. The shrine's '''parishioners (ujiko) include residents of Kosuge 1-chome to 3-chome''', while '''Kosuge 4-chome falls under the jurisdiction of Katori Shrine in Kamichiba'''.
== '''History'''
Kosuge Shrine was founded in '''1869 (Meiji 2)''' to serve as the '''guardian shrine of Kosuge Prefecture''' (小菅県), a short-lived administrative unit established in '''the early Meiji era''' to govern former '''shogunate territories''' and '''hatamoto lands''' in the eastern outskirts of Edo (modern Tokyo). The shrine was originally located '''within the Kosuge Prefectural Office''', which was situated '''at the site of today's Tokyo Detention House'''.<ref name="katsushika1981">Katsushika City Board of Education, *Report on Shrines in Katsushika Ward*, 1981, pp. 43-44.</ref>
In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''
The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery>
File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine
</gallery>
== '''Access'''
* '''15-minute walk from Ayase Station''' ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).
== '''See Also'''
* [[Shinto]]
* [[Tokyo Detention House]]
* [[Amaterasu]]
* [[Katori Shrine (Katsushika)]]
== '''References'''
{{Reflist}}
== '''External Links'''
* {{Commons category|Kosuge Shrine (Katsushika)}}

The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
== '''Access'''== * '''15-minute walk from Ayase Station''' ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小菅神社 |画像=[[File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[葛飾区]][[小菅 (葛飾区)|小菅]]3-1-2 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''小菅神社'''（こすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[葛飾区]]の[[神社]]。 氏子地域は小菅1丁目〜3丁目。なお4丁目は[[香取神社 (葛飾区堀切)|上千葉香取神社]]の氏子にあたる。
== 歴史 == [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）に創建された。[[江戸]]東郊外の旧[[天領]]や旗本領によって構成された[[小菅県]]の守護神とすべく、[[伊勢神宮]]より分霊を勧請したものである。場所は小菅県庁内（現[[東京拘置所]]）に置かれた<ref name="神社">葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年、43-44p</ref>。
[[1871年]]（明治4年）、小菅県が廃止となったため、県庁内に置かれた神社は、地元の小菅村の鎮守だった田中稲荷神社に移設し、「小菅大神宮」に改称された。[[1909年]]（明治42年）、「小菅神社」に改称された<ref name="神社"></ref>。
元々あった田中稲荷神社は当社の[[摂社]]となった。田中稲荷神社は[[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に開村した小菅村の鎮守として創建され、[[正覚寺 (葛飾区小菅)|正覚寺]]が[[別当寺]]であった。なぜ「'''小菅'''稲荷神社」ではなく、「'''田中'''稲荷神社」なのかは謎である。一説によれば、勧請したのが田中氏だからといわれている<ref name="神社"></ref>。 <gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|田中稲荷神社 </gallery>
== 交通アクセス == *[[綾瀬駅]]より徒歩15分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=経路案内}}）。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *葛飾区教育委員会社会教育課 編『葛飾区神社調査報告』葛飾区教育委員会、1981年
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こすけしんしや}} [[Category:葛飾区の神社]] [[Category:小菅]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kosuge-jinja (Katsushika)]][[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852604]] Wikidata name: Kosuge-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852604]] Wikidata name: Kosuge-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kosuge Shrine | native_name = 小菅神社 | image = File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG | image_size = 250px | caption = Main shrine building | map_type = Japan Tokyo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.75469|N|139.82128|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 3-1-2 Kosuge, [[:en:Katsushika, Tokyo|Katsushika]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-Ōmikami]] | established = [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2) | former_names = Kosuge Daijingū | affiliated_shrines = [[Tanaka Inari Shrine]] (subsidiary shrine) | prefecture= {{ill|Kosuge|qid=Q11463521}} }}
'''Kosuge Shrine''' ({{nihongo|小菅神社|Kosuge Jinja}}) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kosuge, Katsushika Ward, Tokyo, Japan'''. The shrine's '''parishioners (ujiko) include residents of Kosuge 1-chome to 3-chome''', while '''Kosuge 4-chome falls under the jurisdiction of Katori Shrine in Kamichiba'''.
== '''History'''=== Kosuge Shrine was founded in '''1869 (Meiji 2)''' to serve as the '''guardian shrine of Kosuge Prefecture''' (小菅県), a short-lived administrative unit established in '''the early Meiji era''' to govern former '''shogunate territories''' and '''hatamoto lands''' in the eastern outskirts of Edo (modern Tokyo). The shrine was originally located '''within the Kosuge Prefectural Office''', which was situated '''at the site of today's Tokyo Detention House'''.<ref name="katsushika1981">Katsushika City Board of Education, *Report on Shrines in Katsushika Ward*, 1981, pp. 43-44.</ref>
In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
== '''Access'''== * '''15-minute walk from Ayase Station''' ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).
== '''See Also'''== * [[Shinto]] * [[:en:Tokyo Detention House|Tokyo Detention House]] * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Katori Shrine (Katsushika)]]
== '''References'''== {{Reflist}}
== '''External Links'''== * {{Commons category|Kosuge Shrine (Katsushika)}}

In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>

In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kosuge-jinja (Katsushika)]][[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kosuge-jinja (Katsushika)]][[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kosuge-jinja (Katsushika)]][[ja:小菅神社 (葛飾区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852604]] Wikidata name: Kosuge-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852604]] Wikidata name: Kosuge-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kosuge Shrine | native_name = 小菅神社 | image = File:Kosuge Jinja (Katsushika).JPG | image_size = 250px | caption = Main shrine building | map_type = Japan Tokyo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.75469|N|139.82128|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 3-1-2 Kosuge, [[:en:Katsushika, Tokyo|Katsushika]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], Japan | deity = [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-Ōmikami]] | established = [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2) | former_names = Kosuge Daijingū | affiliated_shrines = [[Tanaka Inari Shrine]] (subsidiary shrine) | prefecture= {{ill|Kosuge|WD=Q11463521}} }}
'''Kosuge Shrine''' ({{nihongo|小菅神社|Kosuge Jinja}}) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kosuge, Katsushika Ward, Tokyo, Japan'''. The shrine's '''parishioners (ujiko) include residents of Kosuge 1-chome to 3-chome''', while '''Kosuge 4-chome falls under the jurisdiction of Katori Shrine in Kamichiba'''.
== '''History'''=== Kosuge Shrine was founded in '''1869 (Meiji 2)''' to serve as the '''guardian shrine of Kosuge Prefecture''' (小菅県), a short-lived administrative unit established in '''the early Meiji era''' to govern former '''shogunate territories''' and '''hatamoto lands''' in the eastern outskirts of Edo (modern Tokyo). The shrine was originally located '''within the Kosuge Prefectural Office''', which was situated '''at the site of today's Tokyo Detention House'''.<ref name="katsushika1981">Katsushika City Board of Education, *Report on Shrines in Katsushika Ward*, 1981, pp. 43-44.</ref>
In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
== '''Access'''== * '''15-minute walk from Ayase Station''' ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).

Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>

'''Kosuge Shrine''' ({{nihongo|小菅神社|Kosuge Jinja}}) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kosuge, Katsushika Ward, Tokyo, Japan'''. The shrine's '''parishioners (ujiko) include residents of Kosuge 1-chome to 3-chome''', while '''Kosuge 4-chome falls under the jurisdiction of Katori Shrine in Kamichiba'''.

The shrine was originally located '''within the Kosuge Prefectural Office''', which was situated '''at the site of today's Tokyo Detention House'''.<ref name="katsushika1981">Katsushika City Board of Education, *Report on Shrines in Katsushika Ward*, 1981, pp. 43-44.</ref>
In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>

In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
== '''Access'''== * '''15-minute walk from Ayase Station''' ({{OSM route map|start=35.76221,139.82629|goal=35.75460,139.82123|name=Route map}}).

== '''History'''=== Kosuge Shrine was founded in '''1869 (Meiji 2)''' to serve as the '''guardian shrine of Kosuge Prefecture''' (小菅県), a short-lived administrative unit established in '''the early Meiji era''' to govern former '''shogunate territories''' and '''hatamoto lands''' in the eastern outskirts of Edo (modern Tokyo). In '''1871 (Meiji 4)''', after the '''abolition of Kosuge Prefecture''', the shrine was '''relocated''' to the grounds of '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''', which had been the '''local tutelary shrine of Kosuge Village''' since the '''Keichō era (1596–1615)'''. The shrine was renamed '''Kosuge Daijingū''' (小菅大神宮). In '''1909 (Meiji 42)''', it was officially renamed '''Kosuge Shrine'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
=== '''Tanaka Inari Shrine'''=== The original '''Tanaka Inari Shrine''' (田中稲荷神社) became a '''subsidiary shrine''' (*sessha*) of Kosuge Shrine. Although it served as '''Kosuge Village’s tutelary deity''', its name does not include “Kosuge.” The origin of the name '''"Tanaka"''' remains unclear, but one theory suggests it was named after '''a person named Tanaka who enshrined the deity'''.<ref name="katsushika1981"></ref>
<gallery> File:Tanaka Inari Jinja.JPG|Tanaka Inari Shrine </gallery>
